evidence_rationale: Not an approved drug, but MG-132/proteasome inhibition has colon-cancer cell-line evidence.
Investigational MEK inhibitor with CRC clinical study evidence; not FDA-approved as a marketed drug.
FDA/NCI-listed colorectal cancer therapy; label includes metastatic carcinoma of colon or rectum.
Investigational PLK1 inhibitor with advanced solid-tumor trials, but no strong drug-specific CRC evidence found in this pass.
HSP90 inhibition has CRC cell-line sensitization evidence; CCT-018159 remains investigational.
No sufficient CRC-specific clinical or preclinical treatment evidence found in this pass.
Gamma-secretase/Notch class has CRC relevance, but available CRC clinical evidence is for another GSI rather than avagacestat.
FDA-approved for other BRAF-mutant cancers; CRC case/research evidence exists for MEK/ERK-pathway use but not CRC approval.
Approved in breast cancer settings; CRC evidence found here is biomarker-level ESR1 relevance, not fulvestrant treatment evidence.
No sufficient CRC-specific clinical or preclinical treatment evidence found in this pass.
Mycophenolate products are approved for transplant rejection prophylaxis; colorectal carcinoma-cell evidence suggests resistance/inactivation rather than a clear repurposing signal.
FDA-approved for other cancers and has rectal/metastatic colorectal clinical-study evidence, but not listed as CRC-approved therapy.
Investigational compound with colorectal cancer in-vitro/in-vivo ferroptosis evidence.
Investigational mTORC1/2 inhibitor with colon/CRC preclinical evidence.
Investigational ERK inhibitor with CRC case/research evidence; not CRC-approved.
